decision.rationale: Filler word detection is a sharp, single-dimensional behavioral problem with a measurable ground truth. On-device rule-based ASR eliminates LLM cost and cloud latency, keeping gross margins above 85%. The target user is already spending $200-800 on interview coaching — payment intent is proven, not hypothetical. Yoodli is the closest competitor but operates post-session only, which is a structural gap: feedback delivered after the fact does not rewire behavior during the rep. Real-time haptic cue during practice closes that gap with no new hardware requirement. The 14-day solo ship constraint is met: on-device VAD + filler detection + session log is achievable without a backend. MirrorLoop is viable but therapist channel sales cycles run 6-12 months — too slow for signal. StandupMirror's post-session timing is a core structural compromise, not a feature gap. CueCoach ships fastest, charges soonest, and produces the clearest behavioral outcome.
non_goals: Onboarding flow beyond single permission screen
decision.target_user: Mid-career software engineer or finance analyst, age 25-38, preparing for FAANG or investment bank interviews, spending money on LeetCode premium and resume coaches, aware they have a filler word problem but has no real-time feedback tool today.
decision.positioning: The only interview prep tool that corrects your filler words in the moment, not after. One click per filler. Works with any Bluetooth earbud you already own.

price_points: Free: 3 sessions lifetime, no account required
$9/month recurring: unlimited sessions, weekly progress email
$49 one-time lifetime: unlimited sessions, no subscription
$10 per referred paying user: affiliate payout to resume coaches and career influencers
monetization.why_people_pay: Users are already paying $200-800 for interview coaching and failing interviews that cost them $30k-$100k in annual comp. $9/month is a rounding error against that stakes. The free tier creates behavioral proof in 3 sessions — if filler rate drops visibly, conversion is mechanical. Resume coaches pay nothing; they earn $10/referral, so they become free sales reps. Lifetime at $49 captures one-time buyers and reduces churn risk.

metrics.north_star: Weekly active users who complete at least 2 sessions in a 7-day window (measures habit formation, not just install)
metrics.activation: User completes first full session with at least 1 filler cue fired within 24 hours of install
metrics.retention: Percentage of users who complete a session in week 2 after completing one in week 1 (target: 40% at 30 days)
metrics.revenue: Monthly recurring revenue from paid subscribers; secondary: lifetime license units sold per week
ops: Filler detection latency p95 under 200ms on iPhone 12 or newer
False positive rate on filler detection under 5% (measured on internal test corpus)
Weekly progress email open rate above 30%
Affiliate referral conversion rate (clicks to paid) above 8%

must_have: On-device VAD and rule-based filler word detection (um, uh, like, you know, so, basically) with under 200ms latency
Single Bluetooth haptic/audio cue fired per filler event
Session log: filler count, filler per minute, session duration
Chart of filler rate across last 10 sessions visible in app
3-session free tier enforced by local storage, no account required
Paywall after session 3 with $9/month and $49 lifetime options
iOS only, iPhone 12 or newer, iOS 16+
Single microphone permission screen as entire onboarding
Weekly progress email (manual send via Mailchimp for first 100 users, then automated)
nice_later: AI interviewer mode with question prompts
Structured interview question bank by role/company
ship_criteria: Filler detection fires correctly on 10 manually recorded test phrases with no missed detections and under 3 false positives
Bluetooth cue latency under 200ms measured on AirPods Pro on iPhone 14
Session log persists across app restarts
Paywall blocks session 4 and accepts payment via Apple In-App Purchase
At least 5 non-developer users complete a session and report the cue fired when expected
App passes App Store review and is live on TestFlight
Weekly email sent to at least 1 real user with filler rate chart
